prompt: Build a CampusFit Learning & Inventory Operations system with main features including offline user accounts, fitness goal management, study/review planning, inbound receiving workflows, master data management, and a unified notification center. The Vue.js web interface serves three roles: Regular User (students/trainees), Warehouse/Operations Staff, and Administrator. Regular Users sign up and sign in with a local username and password, then complete a fitness assessment (height in ft/in, weight in lb, optional body measurements) and set goals such as Lose 12 lb in 12 weeks or Improve flexibility over 8 weeks, with phase milestones displayed on a dashboard and progress feedback shown as weekly check-ins. They also create study and memorization plans tied to locally managed academic structures (term, school, major, class, course), track daily completion, and tag forgetting points after reviews; the UI provides reminders, streak indicators, and a one-click export to a file for cross-account migration. Operations Staff use a receiving workspace for purchase, transfer, and return receipts: they scan or enter an inbound reference, record quantities, run a pass/fail quality inspection with notes, accept system putaway recommendations, and resolve discrepancies through guided prompts before completion. Administrators manage academic master data with bulk Excel/CSV import/export, review validation errors inline, merge duplicates, and view change history. A messaging center within the UI consolidates announcements, reminders, and follow-ups with read receipts and delivery status visible to authorized users; delivery is in-app only in offline mode, while email/SMS/WeCom are configurable but disabled unless explicitly enabled in a connected environment. The backend uses Spring Boot to implement REST-style APIs consumed by the Vue.js client, enforcing a decoupled architecture where all actions are validated server-side and persisted in MySQL for offline-first local deployment. Authentication uses salted password hashing (bcrypt) with lockout after 5 failed attempts for 15 minutes and session timeout after 30 minutes of inactivity; role-based access control limits administrative actions, inbound posting/unposting, moderation, and report handling to authorized roles. Inbound workflow processing is state-machine-driven (Draft -> Receiving -> Inspection -> Putaway -> Completed/Rejected) with automatic posting only when required fields are satisfied; discrepancy rules include quantity variance thresholds (e.g., >2% or >5 units requires supervisor review) and mandatory reason codes. Fitness goals apply dynamic adjustment rules such as recalculating weekly targets if two consecutive check-ins are missed, while preserving an audit trail. Master data versioning stores effective dates (MM/DD/YYYY) and blocks deletion when referenced by plans; merges preserve the surviving record with a complete change audit. High-performance querying is supported through MySQL partitioning by term and month, pre-aggregated tables for dashboards, result caching with a 5-minute TTL, pagination defaults of 25 rows per page, async loading for heavy reports, and built-in slow-query logging with an admin performance screen. All personal metrics are encrypted at rest using AES-256, sensitive fields are masked in logs and exports by default, and exports require a password-protected file with a configurable retention policy (default 30 days) plus full account deletion that hard-deletes identifiers while retaining de-identified audit events for compliance.
